situation: audit-2026-07-03-012 finding SCRIPTS-M02 (pending, priority 2, route-fix): parallel-merge.sh line 78 fallback 'git reset --hard HEAD@{1}' can destroy uncommitted WIP when merge --abort fails. Scenario: merge commits successfully but integration test fails afterward — merge --abort is a no-op (no in-progress merge), falls through to reset --hard HEAD@{1}, which may reference an unrelated reflog entry if other operations occurred between merge start and test failure.
action: Pin PRE_MERGE_HEAD=$(git rev-parse HEAD) immediately before the git merge attempt. Both rollback paths (test-fail at line 78 and conflict at line 89) now use 'git reset --hard $PRE_MERGE_HEAD' as fallback instead of HEAD@{1}. Guarantees rollback never rewinds past the pre-merge safe point regardless of reflog state.
